mnemonics: DATE: Display And Time Environment - Use the 'date' command to Display the system's current time and date, And format it to show Time in various Environments.
keyPoints: The 'date' command displays or sets the system date and time.
It can format the output using a wide variety of format specifiers (e.g., +%Y-%m-%d).
Only the superuser (root) can set the system date and time.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the '+' sign when specifying a format string (e.g., using 'date %Y' instead of 'date +%Y').
Attempting to set the date without superuser privileges, which results in a 'date: cannot set date: Operation not permitted' error.
